description:
To properly set the permissions of /var/log/syslog, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0640 /var/log/syslog
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/syslog</tt> file contains logs of error messages in
the system and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /var/log/syslog, run the command: 
$ ls -l /var/log/syslog
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: -rw-r-----
title: Verify Permissions on /var/log/syslog File
